ddls:
CREATE TABLE "memory_configurations" (
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each memory configuration */,
  "native_data_width" INTEGER /* Native data width in bits */,
  "supported_memory_sizes" TEXT /* Supported memory sizes and configurations (e.g., 8K / (2,048x32)) */,
  "num_bram_primitives" INTEGER /* Number of BRAM primitives required (18Kbit each) */,
  "bram_addr_bits_required" INTEGER /* Number of BRAM address bits required */,
  "bram_addr_bit_usage" TEXT /* Typical bit usage for BRAM addresses */,
  "clock_frequency" TEXT /* Clock frequency of the FPGA design (in MHz) */,
  "power_consumption" TEXT /* Power consumption of the FPGA design (in Watts) */,
  "device_family" TEXT /* Device family of the FPGA (e.g., Virtex, Kintex, etc.) */,
  "device_speed_grade" TEXT /* Speed grade of the FPGA device (e.g., -1, -2, etc.) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("config_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "bram_primitives" (
  "bram_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each BRAM primitive */,
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the memory configuration */,
  "bram_size" TEXT /* Size of the BRAM primitive (e.g., 18Kbit) */,
  "bram_type" TEXT /* Type of the BRAM primitive (e.g., BRAM18K) */,
  "bram_latency" INTEGER /* Latency of the BRAM primitive (in clock cycles) */,
  "bram_init_file" TEXT /* Initialization file for the BRAM primitive (e.g., .coe file) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("bram_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_bram_primitives_config_id FOREIGN KEY ("config_id") REFERENCES memory_configurations ("config_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., engineer, project manager, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user (e.g., design, verification, etc.) */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Job title of the user (e.g., senior engineer, lead engineer, etc.) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "config_id" INTEGER /* ID of the memory configuration being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the configuration */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the configuration was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "access_ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user accessing the configuration */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_config_id FOREIGN KEY ("config_id") REFERENCES memory_configurations ("config_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "config_id" INTEGER /* ID of the memory configuration for which the report was generated */,
  "report_name" TEXT /* Name of the report */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of report (e.g., summary, detailed) */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date when the report was generated */,
  "report_file" TEXT /* File name of the report (e.g., pdf, csv, etc.) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_config_id FOREIGN KEY ("config_id") REFERENCES memory_configurations ("config_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "analysis_results" (
  "analysis_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each analysis */,
  "config1_id" INTEGER /* ID of the first memory configuration being compared */,
  "config2_id" INTEGER /* ID of the second memory configuration being compared */,
  "analysis_type" TEXT /* Type of analysis (e.g., power consumption, area optimization) */,
  "analysis_date" TEXT /* Date when the analysis was performed */,
  "analysis_result" TEXT /* Result of the analysis (e.g., config1 is better than config2) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analysis_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_analysis_results_config2_id FOREIGN KEY ("config2_id") REFERENCES memory_configurations ("config_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_analysis_results_config1_id FOREIGN KEY ("config1_id") REFERENCES memory_configurations ("config_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user with this role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., engineer, project manager, admin) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_roles_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
